title: reichardia gaditana subsp. gaditana
intro: Reichardia gaditana subsp. gaditana, also known as Spanish needle or Cretan hawk's beard, is a plant species in the dandelion family that is native to the Iberian Peninsula. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and has been employed to treat various health issues such as digestive problems and skin conditions. The plant is rich in flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that provide several health benefits, including protecting against oxidative stress and promoting wound healing. Reichardia gaditana subsp. gaditana can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal purposes, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects.
